text.title: JavaScript/TypeScript Stack Practices Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the practices-js-ts-stack methodology. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: tsconfig MUST set strict=true, noUncheckedIndexedAccess=true, noImplicitOverride=true, exactOptionalPropertyTypes=true.
rule.rationale: Default strictness still allows arr[0] to be typed as T (not T | undefined); noUncheckedIndexedAccess closes that hole. exactOptionalPropertyTypes catches accidental undefined assignments.

rule.statement: package.json MUST set "type": "module" and use an "exports" map. CommonJS require() in source is forbidden; use dynamic import() if interop is required.
rule.rationale: ESM is the Node + browser standard; mixed CJS/ESM trees produce dual-package hazard. Pinning type=module + exports map prevents the dual-package state.

rule.statement: ESLint MUST use the flat-config format (eslint.config.js). The legacy .eslintrc cascade is forbidden in new projects.
rule.rationale: Flat config is the supported format from ESLint 9; the legacy cascade is being removed and double-configures projects that mix them.

rule.statement: CI MUST run a tsc --noEmit step distinct from build. Skipping typecheck because the build emits types is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Bundlers (esbuild, tsup, swc) skip typecheck by default for speed. Without a dedicated --noEmit step, type errors land in main unnoticed.

rule.statement: Prettier handles formatting; ESLint MUST NOT include stylistic rules that overlap with Prettier (use eslint-config-prettier). Two formatters fighting each other are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Overlapping rules produce ping-pong autofixes and break CI gates.

rule.statement: @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any and no-unused-vars MUST be errors (not warnings). Local exceptions require an // eslint-disable-next-line comment with a reason.
rule.rationale: Warnings get ignored; errors block CI. Requiring a reason comment makes exceptions reviewable.
